results.justification: Dynamic per-layer per-token context-priority attention mask covers tier-asymmetric mask paradigm.
Sliding-window adaptation covers tier-2 active-context window vs tier-3 broadcast.

results.matched_effect_cluster: Mixture attention spans heterogeneous sliding windows
results.justification: Per-head heterogeneous sliding-window lengths covers per-tier attention granularity.

results.matched_effect_cluster: Hierarchical attention L1 system-prompt always present
results.justification: Hierarchical L1-always-present + L2/L3 active/background tiering IS the TONGKONAN-TIER-GATE structure.

results.matched_effect_cluster: HatLLM hierarchical attention masking per-layer regime
results.justification: EXACT TWIN — HatLLM 3-regime hierarchical attention masking (intra-item, vanilla, cross-item) IS the 3-tier-asymmetric attention mask paradigm of TONGKONAN-TIER-GATE.
Hierarchical context-window with L1 always-present system tier IS the tier-3 broadcast mechanism.
Gated attention with hybrid mixing covers tiered attention mechanism.

results.matched_effect_cluster: Efficient attention mechanisms masking sparse survey
results.justification: Efficient-attention survey covers tiered masking + sparse attention designs.
